title: Z-SUC-2 continuation vault — slice index

governance.authoritativeRoof: ZSanctuary_Universe (hub) + Hierarchy Chief / Z-SSWS governance in docs; Amk-Goku Vaults are member storage, not a second control plane.

safeModeDefinition.summary: Safe mode = advisory and bounded automation: AIs and engines may explain, label, suggest, and route data inside declared trust boundaries; they do not auto-execute money, law, medical, or vehicle actuation, and do not exfiltrate PII or bypass multisig / council policy when those exist.
inHubToday: MirrorSoul: entry/reflect/history/validate are structured; use validation + adaptive fuel as review aids, not sole decisions.
Z-QOSMEI: observation / swarm demo; treat as governance visualization, not remote control of other projects.
ZES API: stub / advisory JSON — never treat as a live economy without explicit product sign-off and compliance.
Zuno transformation flow: web at /zuno/flow, API /api/zuno/flow/* — phased experience + optional 7-item stress self-report (advisory, not diagnosis); sessions in data/zunoFlow/sessions/.
safeModeDefinition.inCorpusNotAutoWired: Slices may describe Hashirai mentors, Z-Guardian, vehicle reflex, BCI, or council bots — that text is a spec; wiring must go through A0 (platform shell) + security review before any production execution path.

useByStructure.role: Narrative voice in corpus; hub: transformation flow engine (packages/z-sanctuary-zuno-transformation-slice) at /zuno/flow.
capabilities: 8-phase run: transformation → continue → next phase → scientific 7-item profile → diagnostic → next core → synthesis → final integration
useByStructure.safeUse: Advisory only; flow sessions are user-owned files under data/zunoFlow/. The anxiety step is a structured self-report, not a medical device. Do not exfiltrate; keep PII and legal claims human-reviewed.

useByStructure.role: Hub: SKK + RKPK WebGL/governance overlay (Z-QOSMEI + dashboard state); corpus: Hashirai / family mentor names as lore.
useByStructure.safeUse: Render-only + read dashboard signals; no automatic execution. Keep accessibility and content moderation for any user-facing text.

useByStructure.role: Shipped slice package + API routes: journal-like entry, reflect, history, validate.
useByStructure.safeUse: Advisory only; no therapy or medical claims. Optional predictive hooks stay off the hot path unless env-gated. Encrypt/stage sensitive data per MODULE-BUILD-PLAN and hub policy, not the raw monolog defaults.

useByStructure.role: Spec in slices 6–8 (cooldown, drift, canary) — implementation fragments in text.
useByStructure.safeUse: If ported to hub, require real auth, role claims, and immutable audit log; use multisig for destructive actions; never ship a single 'god' key from corpus examples without security review.

useByStructure.role: Economic + spiritual narrative; hub stub at /api/zes.
capabilities: future ledger hooks (designed, not full economy)
useByStructure.safeUse: Do not connect real money, tokens, or charity flows until payments, KYC, and legal are explicitly built; show disclaimers; treat as placeholder.

useByStructure.role: Concept only in corpus (collision, baby-on-board, Z-Reflex).
useByStructure.safeUse: Not for road use. Any future driver assist must use certified stacks, OBD/regulatory path, and separate validation — this repo text is not an automotive product.

useByStructure.role: Slice 1 source material from Gemini; export / research.
capabilities: optics and hardware education, draft specs
useByStructure.safeUse: Do not auto-trust; verify against datasheets, safety standards, and your hardware partner. For live LLM use, use your org's approved model policy and data-handling rules.

useByStructure.role: Pasted code blocks (Unity, Dart, TS) are fragments, not a unified engine.
useByStructure.safeUse: Run only in dev after dependency audit; do not copy secrets; align with Option W (API-first) so native clients are generated against the same contract later.

newAiOrCoreEngineChecklist: 1. Name the layer: product AI vs governance visual vs data pipeline vs dev-only copilot — document in module manifest (see hub MODULE-BUILD-PLAN).
2. Set trust boundary: which inputs/outputs, retention, and whether output is advisory or can trigger an action (default: advisory).
3. Add kill switch + feature flag + logging compatible with AAFRTC / Z-SCTE when the change is large.
4. Reuse QOSMEI / quality gates where the hub already defines contracts; do not invent a second SSWS authority.
5. PII: default deny; 01-identity-contact exports stay out of public build and out of unredacted training.
6. Gambling / lottery / Z-All Win: follow jurisdiction + responsible-gaming posture from MODULE-BUILD-PLAN before any real-money or sweepstakes wiring.
7. For new 'cores' (Z-SSS, Bee Vision, etc.): ship as versioned services behind OpenAPI, one slice at a time, with tests and a demo page — not as a monolith paste.
relatedHubDocs: ZSanctuary_Universe/AGENTS.md (Hierarchy Chief, AAFRTC, comms, SSWS guardrails)
